title: Record canonical transactions
description: Record exact immutable source facts and provenance for a transaction on an active owned account. Implements G2 and UC-LEDGER-002 under A1-A4 and A12.
rationale: Trustworthy actuals require precise signed amounts, dates, source identity, and provenance rather than normalized or silently edited values.
story_want: record a precise transaction from an owned account
story_so_that: the original financial fact can always be reproduced and reconciled
acceptance_criteria: Given an active account and valid original description, exact base-10 signed amount, ZAR currency, transaction date, source reference, provenance, and idempotency identity, when the transaction is recorded, then the Ledger returns one stable transaction containing those values without precision loss.
Given a transaction with a distinct supplied posting date, when it is recorded, then both transaction date and posting date round-trip without substituting one for the other.

Given any missing required fact, unsupported currency, zero amount, malformed date, or archived account, when recording is attempted, then the Ledger returns a stable validation error and creates no transaction.

Given a recorded transaction, when it is read later, then its account identity, original description, signed amount, currency, supplied dates, source reference, provenance, and stable identifier are unchanged.

Given a movement on an active owned asset or liability account, when its signed amount is recorded, then a positive value means the owner economic position improved and a negative value means it worsened: asset increases and liability reductions are positive; asset decreases and liability increases are negative.
security_criteria: Returned and diagnostic data must not expose unmasked account identifiers or credentials.
